text.title: Stakeholder Engagement Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent invents stakeholder names not found in org-chart or charter inputs.
antipattern.fix: Reject any name not in org-chart export unless flagged suggested_to_verify.

antipattern.symptom: Agent sends engagement communications directly to executives without human review.
antipattern.root-cause: Tone, timing, and political context require human judgement.
antipattern.fix: Block agent send to exec/regulator/customer/board; only draft.

antipattern.symptom: Bulk reclassification of stakeholder quadrants in a single agent run.
antipattern.root-cause: Bulk changes destroy evidence trail and reverse carefully built relationships.
antipattern.fix: Cap agent edits to 5 stakeholders per run; require human review on diff.

antipattern.symptom: Agent reads polite email tone as 'supportive' attitude.
antipattern.fix: Require behavioural-signal triangulation; reject attitude updates without quoted evidence.
